definition.name: normal_state
definition.definition: A state φ on a von Neumann algebra M ⊂ B(H) is called normal if it is continuous in the ultraweak (σ-weak) topology, equivalently if φ(T) = Tr(ρT) for some density operator ρ (a positive trace-class operator with Tr(ρ) = 1) on H. The normal state space S_n(M) is the set of all normal states of M. For B(H) with H separable, every state on B(H) that is σ-weakly continuous is normal, and the normal states are exactly the density operators. In the face-commutant correspondence, the face F_{S(A)}(φ) is affinely isomorphic to the normal state space of the von Neumann algebra π_φ(A)'' = π_φ(A)̄^{WOT}. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §III.2.
